optional-columns.label: Optional columns
optional-columns.summary: Learn how to insert data into tables with optional columns, using NULL and default values in SQL.

q1.title: Insert a row with only required columns

q2.title: Insert with explicit NULL for an optional column

q1.title: Insert a row with only required columns
q1.prompt: Insert a new item into `inventory_items` with id 10, name 'Mini Poster', category 'Decor', and price 5.00. Do not specify any optional columns. Write the SQL statement.
q1.hint: Focus on the SQL task being asked for, not on copying final query text.

help.concept: Build the query from the operation the exercise is testing.
help.hint_1: Think about which clauses or functions are required for the task.
help.hint_2: Construct the query based on what result the exercise expects, not by repeating exact solution wording.

q2.title: Insert with explicit NULL for an optional column
q2.prompt: Insert a new item into `inventory_items` with id 11, name 'Test Magnet', category 'Accessories', price 2.50, and notes set to NULL. All other columns should use their defaults. Write the SQL statement.
q2.hint: Focus on the SQL task being asked for, not the final solution text.

help.concept: Build the query from the operation the exercise is testing.
help.hint_1: Eliminate choices or interpretations that do not match the task.
help.hint_2: Choose the concept that best fits what the exercise is asking you to do.

q3.title: Insert using DEFAULT keyword
q3.prompt: Insert a new item into `inventory_items` with id 12, name 'Sample Bottle', category 'Kitchen', price 12.00, and status set to its default value using the DEFAULT keyword. All other optional columns should use their defaults. Write the SQL statement.
q3.hint: Focus on the SQL task being asked for, not on copying final query text.

help.concept: Build the query from the operation the exercise is testing.
help.hint_1: Think about which clauses or functions are required for the task.
help.hint_2: Construct the query based on what result the exercise expects, not by repeating exact solution wording.

q4.title: Which columns are optional in inventory_items?
q4.prompt: Select all columns in the `inventory_items` table that are optional when inserting a new row (can be omitted from the column list).
q4.hint: Look for columns that allow NULL or have a DEFAULT value.

help.concept: A column is optional if it allows NULL or has a DEFAULT value. NOT NULL columns without defaults are required.
help.hint_1: Check the table definition for DEFAULT values and NULL allowances.
help.hint_2: Columns like notes (NULL) and status (DEFAULT) are optional.

q5.title: Default value for status
q5.prompt: What is the default value for the `status` column in `inventory_items`?
q5.hint: Check the table definition for the DEFAULT assigned to status.

help.concept: A DEFAULT value is used when no value is provided for a column during insertion.
help.hint_1: Look for the DEFAULT keyword in the CREATE TABLE statement for status.
help.hint_2: The default is a string value set in the schema.

q5.template: The default value for status is [blank].

sk1.title: What are optional columns?
sk1.bodyMarkdown:
In SQL, some columns in a table can be optional, meaning you don't have to provide a value for them when inserting a new row. These columns either allow NULL values or have a DEFAULT value defined. For example, in the `inventory_items` table, the `notes` column is optional because it allows NULL, and the `status`, `is_test`, and `last_updated` columns have default values.

```sql
CREATE TABLE inventory_items (
  id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
  name TEXT NOT NULL,
  category TEXT NOT NULL,
  price REAL NOT NULL,
  status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'draft',
  is_test INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  notes TEXT NULL,
  last_updated TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '2026-02-01'
);
```

sk2.title: Inserting with optional columns
sk2.bodyMarkdown:
When inserting data, you can skip optional columns in your column list. SQL will use the DEFAULT value if defined, or NULL if the column allows it. For example, to insert a new item without specifying `notes` or `status`:

```sql
INSERT INTO inventory_items (id, name, category, price) VALUES (7, 'Desk Organizer', 'Accessories', 8.50);
```
This will set `status` to 'draft', `is_test` to 0, `notes` to NULL, and `last_updated` to '2026-02-01' for the new row.

sk3.title: Explicit NULL and DEFAULT values
sk3.bodyMarkdown:
You can also explicitly set a column to NULL (if allowed) or to its default value using the DEFAULT keyword:

```sql
INSERT INTO inventory_items (id, name, category, price, notes) VALUES (8, 'Sample Keychain', 'Accessories', 3.00, NULL);
```
Or, to use the default for `status`:

```sql
INSERT INTO inventory_items (id, name, category, price, status) VALUES (9, 'Trial Cup', 'Kitchen', 6.00, DEFAULT);
```
